feat: release workflow with Helm chart and GHCR publishing (#15)

* feat: release workflow with Helm chart and GHCR publishing

Add a release workflow triggered by semantic version tags (v*.*.*):

Release artifacts:
- containerd-shim-cloudhv-v1 (linux/amd64)
- cloudhv-agent (linux/amd64, static musl)
- vmlinux (guest kernel)
- rootfs.ext4 (guest rootfs with crun)
- SHA-256 checksums

OCI artifacts pushed to GHCR:
- ghcr.io/devigned/cloudhv-installer:<tag> (installer image)
- ghcr.io/devigned/charts/cloudhv-installer:<version> (Helm chart)

Helm chart (charts/cloudhv-installer/):
- DaemonSet: installs shim, kernel, rootfs onto nodes
- RuntimeClass: registers cloudhv handler with scheduling and overhead
- Configurable via values.yaml (image, nodeSelector, resources)

Release notes auto-generated from conventional commits between tags,
grouped by type (features, fixes, performance, docs, etc.).

Usage:
  git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0

Fix nginx crash on HAOS: generate self-contained config, avoid all user-switching syscalls (#23)

* Initial plan

* Fix nginx crash on HAOS: self-contained config, run as root, single-process mode

The HA addon container has restricted capabilities (AppArmor nginx_service
sub-profile lacks capability chown/setgid/setuid). Previous attempts failed:
- v1.24.2.1: Permission denied on /var/lib/nginx paths
- v1.24.2.2: chown to UID 100 (nginx) blocked
- v1.24.2.3: s6-setuidgid/initgroups blocked

Fix: Generate a self-contained nginx.conf that bypasses all Alpine defaults.
Use `user root;` to avoid chown/setuid to the nginx user, `access_log off;`
to avoid the compiled-in access log path, `-e stderr` to redirect early
startup logs, and `master_process off;` to run in single-process mode
(no worker forking, no initgroups/setgid calls).

Tested with --cap-drop=ALL --cap-add=NET_BIND_SERVICE to verify it works
even with severely restricted capabilities.

Co-authored-by: Crafter-Y <71288761+Crafter-Y@users.noreply.github.com>

* Set restrictive permissions (700) on /tmp/nginx runtime directory

* fix: detect tool messages in Claude SDK assistant message content

The previous gate required typeof msg.name === 'string' at the top
level, but Claude SDK tool activity is carried in assistant messages
as message.content[].type === 'tool_use' blocks — so the name field
is nested, not top-level, and the log never fired.

Fix by first checking for tool_use/tool_result blocks inside
message.content, then falling back to direct type checks and the
name+args/result shape for other formats (e.g. vincent_cli).

commit.message:
fix: make MessageQueue thread-safe with SynchronizedMessageQueue wrapper (#347)

## Problem

`AgentSessionInfo.MessageQueue` is a plain `List<string>` accessed
concurrently from multiple threads without synchronization:

| Thread | Operation | Location |
|--------|-----------|----------|
| WsBridge background | `MessageQueue.Count` read |
WsBridgeServer.cs:994 |
| WsBridge background | `EnqueueMessage()` → `Add()` |
WsBridgeServer.cs:464 |
| Orchestrator Task.WhenAll | `EnqueueMessage()` → `Add()` |
CopilotService.Organization.cs:981 |
| UI thread | Blazor iteration `[i]`, `Count` |
ExpandedSessionView.razor |
| UI thread (via Invoke) | `CompleteResponse` dequeue | Events.cs:966 |

`List<T>` is not thread-safe — concurrent access corrupts its internal
array, causing `ArgumentOutOfRangeException`, silent data loss, or
`InvalidOperationException`.

## Fix

Replace `List<string>` with `SynchronizedMessageQueue` — an
internally-locked wrapper exposing the same operations:

- **`TryDequeue()`** — atomic check-and-remove-first, replaces 3
non-atomic `if (Count > 0) { var x = [0]; RemoveAt(0); }` patterns
- **`TryRemoveAt(index)`** — atomic bounds-check-and-remove, replaces
`if (index >= 0 && index < Count) RemoveAt(index)`
- **`AddAndGetCount()`** — atomic add + count return, keeps image/mode
parallel queues aligned in `EnqueueMessage`
- **`Snapshot()`** — point-in-time copy for safe Blazor iteration
- **`IReadOnlyList<string>`** — backward-compatible enumeration
(enumerator operates on snapshot)

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Models/SynchronizedMessageQueue.cs` | **New** — thread-safe wrapper
with internal lock |
| `Models/AgentSessionInfo.cs` | Type change: `List<string>` →
`SynchronizedMessageQueue` |
| `Services/CopilotService.cs` | `TryDequeue()` in CreateSession drain,
`AddAndGetCount()` in EnqueueMessage, `TryRemoveAt()` in
RemoveQueuedMessage |
| `Services/CopilotService.Events.cs` | `TryDequeue()` in
CompleteResponse and reflection follow-up dispatch |
| `Components/ExpandedSessionView.razor` | `Snapshot()` for iteration |
| `Components/Pages/Dashboard.razor` | `TryRemoveAt()` in local
RemoveQueuedMessage |
| `Tests/SynchronizedMessageQueueTests.cs` | **New** — 12 tests
including concurrent stress |
| `Tests/PolyPilot.Tests.csproj` | Compile include for new model |

## Testing

- All 2411 existing tests pass (3 pre-existing CliPath failures
unrelated)
- 12 new tests: basic ops, TryDequeue, TryRemoveAt, AddAndGetCount,
Snapshot isolation, concurrent add/dequeue, concurrent
add/clear/snapshot stress

Closes #346

commit.message:
Filter empty string args to prevent node from hanging (#2328)

When `"args": ""` is set in launch.json, `asArray("")` converts it to
`[""]` — a single-element array containing an empty string. This empty
string is passed as a literal argument to node via runInTerminal, and
node interprets `""` as "read script from stdin", causing it to hang
indefinitely with all breakpoints unbound.

Filter out empty strings from the args array before constructing the
node command line.

Fixes https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/10764876

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix $0 in path parameter values treated as regex back-reference (#310)

* Fix $0 in path parameter values treated as regex back-reference (#141)

Regex.Replace uses $0, $1, $& etc. as back-references in the replacement
string. When a path parameter value contained a literal dollar sign (e.g.
"$0something"), Regex.Replace would substitute it with the matched text
instead of the literal value.

Fix: escape $ characters in the replacement string by replacing "$" with
"$$" (the .NET replacement-string escape for a literal dollar sign).
This applies to both the v2 SwaggerClientProvider and v3 OpenApiClientProvider.

Also adds:
- EchoPathController in the test server that returns its string path parameter

* Add concurrency group to CI workflow to cancel redundant builds (#309)

When multiple commits are pushed to the same PR branch, GitHub Actions
queues a new build for each push. Without a concurrency group, the older
builds keep running even though they're now stale.

Adding cancel-in-progress: true cancels the in-progress build when a
newer commit arrives on the same ref, reducing CI minutes consumed and
giving faster feedback on the latest commit.

fix(ci): disable persist-credentials so app token is used for push (#28)

actions/checkout configures git auth via an extraheader with the
default GITHUB_TOKEN. This extraheader takes precedence over the
remote URL credentials set by git remote set-url, so the push still
uses the unprivileged GITHUB_TOKEN and gets blocked by branch
protection.

Setting persist-credentials: false prevents checkout from leaving
the GITHUB_TOKEN extraheader in git config, allowing the app token
embedded in the remote URL to be used for the push.

fix: eliminate copy_bytes_raw from HTTP body reading

- http_read_body_small: read directly into pre-allocated buffer
  instead of copy_bytes_raw + string concatenation per chunk
- http_read_request body: same direct-read approach
- http_url_decode/http_json_escape/http_json_unescape: use
  pre-allocated buffer with string indexing instead of store8

Reduces heap allocations per request from hundreds to single digits.

commit.message:
fix: rewrite net_read_line and net_read_all with pre-allocated buffers

- net_read_line: use make_buffer + string indexing instead of
  per-character copy_bytes_raw (eliminates O(n) allocations per line)
- net_read_all: use make_buffer + read_byte copy instead of
  repeated copy_bytes_raw + string concatenation

These changes dramatically reduce heap allocation pressure for
HTTP header parsing (previously ~300 allocations per request).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(LOOP-ID-003/004): analyze ObjectIdCache syncIdentifier mapping

ObjectIdCache maps Loop syncIdentifier → Nightscout ObjectId:
- add(): Store mapping after POST
- findObjectIdBySyncIdentifier(): Lookup for UPDATE/DELETE
- purge(): Remove entries older than date

Cache expiration can cause duplicates if treatment re-uploaded
after purge (no ObjectId to reference for upsert).

PR #8447/Option G helps by promoting UUID to identifier field,
reducing dependence on volatile cache.

Refs: REQ-SYNC-072, GAP-SYNC-005

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: eliminate pipe trick from make_buffer and copy_bytes_raw

- make_buffer: use null-terminator truncation instead of copy_bytes_raw
  (always force heap copy via double to avoid corrupting string literals)
- copy_bytes_raw: rewrite with read_byte + string indexing loop
  instead of pipe() syscall (eliminates pipe fd overhead entirely)

The pipe trick creates kernel pipe objects for every byte copy, causing
the 1MB Novus heap to fill up from intermediate allocations. These
changes reduce heap pressure by 10-100x for typical operations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: eliminate pipe_copy from all MySQL operations

- Replace my_buf_copy pipe_copy with byte-by-byte string indexing
- Add my_extract() for safe buffer extraction without pipe/copy_bytes_raw
- Replace all copy_bytes_raw calls with my_extract (text query + prepared stmt)
- Rewrite my_read_exact with inline read syscall (no pipe, no intermediate buffer)
- Rewrite mysql_read_packet with inline read + string indexing for header
- Fix my_write_cstr to use string indexing instead of store8
- Remove debug prints from mysql_query

These changes eliminate hundreds of pipe() syscalls per query, fixing
crashes caused by the 1MB Novus heap filling up from pipe-trick allocations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Agentic Workflow Executor — AI agent drives execution adaptively

New agentic-executor.ts replaces mechanical step execution:
- LLM receives the workflow plan as a starting template
- After each step, LLM sees the result and DECIDES:
  - Continue as planned
  - Modify next step params (focus on specific user/entity)
  - Add new investigation steps not in the original plan
  - Skip remaining steps if findings don't warrant more
  - Produce analysis when ready (not just at the end)
- Max 15 tool calls (planned + agent-added), 20 iterations
- Skills + remediation KB injected into agent context

Three execution modes now available:
- ▶ Run All Steps: mechanical, runs every step in order
- 🤖 Run with AI Agent: agentic, adapts based on findings
- 🧠 Analyze with AI: post-execution analysis only

UI additions:
- '🤖 Run with AI Agent' button alongside 'Run All Steps'
- Agent activity log: shows decisions, added steps, skipped steps
  with color-coded icons (➕ added, ⏭️ skipped, 🤔 thinking)
- Agent-added steps marked in log with purple badge

API: new 'execute-agentic' action on /api/workflows

Test: agentic execution test verifying agent adapts the plan,
tracks added steps, and produces analysis with verdict + risk score
